title: Key concepts: the spread of Islam
description: Check your understanding of the first Islamic empires.
topic_path: Key concepts: the spread of Islam

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following is true about conversions to Islam? Select all that apply. 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The bulk of conversions did not happen immediately after the advent of Islam.

choices.content: They happened mostly during the reign of the Rashidun.
They occurred for a multitude of reasons
Conversions reached their height during the late Abbasid caliphate

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following factors resulted in weaker Byzantine and Sassanian empires? Select all that apply.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The two civilizations had longstanding tensions with one another.

choices.content: A long period of fighting with one another
Attacks by neighboring nomadic groups groups from the Steppe region

assessment_items.question_content:
Which caliphate was not dynastic?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The first caliphate was not dynastic.

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following is a reason for the decline of Abbasid power?

 [[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lower tax revenues due to conversion to Islam.
Disagreements over succession of the caliph

assessment_items.question_content:
Why were Sufi missionaries especially successful at spreading Islam?

 [[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The success of Sufi missionaries did not have to do with the government.

choices.content: They had the support of powerful states
They shared Islamic ideas through locally accessible ideas
They were able to gather wealth through military conquest

assessment_items.question_content:
Why would conversion to Islam from Christianity and Judaism be easier than from other faiths?

 [[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam are sometimes described together as the Abrahamic Faiths

choices.content: Christians and Jews spoke the same language as Muslims
Muslim rulers gave Christians and Jews special incentives to convert
